refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and derivation method; (c) history log captures all updates with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable; (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) and operational parameters.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context using defined derivation rules.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability links from worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline records → history entries.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Requirements Engine (PRE) module that ingests mission context via API, applies rule-based derivation logic, persists baselines to a versioned database, and maintains an immutable audit log.
DP-B: Distributed derivation where each vehicle computes its own performance requirements locally and syncs to a central repository with eventual consistency.
DP-C: Manual performance requirement entry by Site Manager with system-assisted validation and history tracking via a web portal.
DP-D: Hybrid approach: PRE engine derives requirements; Site Manager reviews and approves; system records baseline and maintains history with role-based access control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid Performance Requirements Management System combining automated derivation (PRE engine) with human review/approval (Site Manager gate) and centralized versioned baseline storage with immutable audit log. This balances automation, safety oversight, and traceability.

potential_sources: DP-D couples performance derivation logic (PRE engine) to mission context ingestion (worksite inputs) and to baseline persistence (database). These are logically separable but functionally interdependent.
DP-D couples Site Manager approval workflow to baseline recording; this is intentional for safety criticality and is a weak coupling (approval is a gate, not a tight algorithmic dependency).
DP-D does not couple to vehicle-level control or teleoperation; it is a planning/management layer.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is acceptable and intentional. The PRE engine is a cohesive module; approval workflow is a separate concern (can be implemented as a state machine). Database versioning is a standard pattern. No problematic circular dependencies or hidden couplings detected.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in a structured format (e.g., JSON, database schema) that the PRE engine can parse.
ASSUMPTION: Derivation rules (e.g., cycle time = f(terrain, payload, vehicle capability)) are well-defined and can be encoded as deterministic logic.
ASSUMPTION: Site Manager has authority and availability to review and approve derived requirements in a timely manner (not a bottleneck).
RISK: If derivation rules are incomplete or incorrect, derived requirements may be infeasible or unsafe. Mitigation: validation rules and Site Manager review gate.
RISK: If audit log is not immutable, compliance and traceability are compromised. Mitigation: use append-only database or blockchain-style versioning.
RISK: If PRE engine is tightly coupled to a specific mission context schema, it may not scale to new worksite types. Mitigation: parameterize derivation rules and use a flexible schema.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the derivation rule set and validation criteria before implementation. Ensure the PRE engine is designed with rule parameterization to support future worksite types. Define the Site Manager approval workflow (e.g., SLA for review, escalation path for conflicts). Specify the audit log schema and immutability guarantees.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary. It contains all subsystems and services required to deliver the NGHE mission. For CR-NGHE-005, the Performance Requirements Engine (PRE) and baseline management system are core components of the NGHE Solution. The PRE ingests worksite mission context, applies derivation logic, and persists baselines. The NGHE Solution also coordinates with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to ensure derived requirements are communicated and enforced.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints, operational policies) flows into the NGHE Solution via the Tasking interface. This is the primary input to the PRE engine.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Derived performance requirements (cycle time targets, energy efficiency targets, availability targets, safety margins) are published by the NGHE Solution via the Telemetry interface to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for execution and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical requirements (e.g., safety margins, emergency stop thresholds) are derived and published via SafetyAlert. Safety Services may also feed back safety constraints or alerts that trigger re-derivation of requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: The NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with Site Manager portal (for approval workflow) and to sync baseline history with central repository (for audit trail and traceability).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for performance requirements management. The PRE engine, baseline storage, and approval workflow are core NGHE functions. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary. High containment because all derivation logic, persistence, and audit trail are internal to NGHE Solution.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing performance targets, not deriving them. Assigning PRE to Vehicle would scatter derivation logic across multiple vehicle instances and break centralized baseline management. Not suitable.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote control and monitoring, not performance requirement derivation. Assigning PRE to Teleoperation would couple derivation to tele-operator workflow and break traceability to worksite mission context. Not suitable.

Safety Services is responsible for safety monitoring and alerts, not performance requirement derivation. While safety margins are part of derived requirements, the full derivation logic (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) is outside Safety Services scope. Partial containment only for safety-specific sub-requirements. Not suitable as primary container.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (System Engineering team or Performance Management subsystem within NGHE Solution)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Requirements Engine and baseline management system are best contained within the NGHE Solution module because: (1) they are core planning/management functions that operate at the system level, not at the vehicle or teleoperation level; (2) they require centralized access to worksite mission context (Tasking interface) and must produce a single source of truth for performance baselines; (3) they must maintain an immutable audit trail for compliance and traceability, which is a system-level concern; (4) they interface with multiple downstream modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) via standard interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert), not via direct coupling.
containment_risks: RISK: If the NGHE Solution module is too large or poorly structured, the PRE engine may become buried and difficult to maintain. Mitigation: define a clear Performance Management subsystem within NGHE Solution with explicit ownership and interfaces.

RISK: If the Tasking interface is not well-defined, the PRE engine may receive incomplete or ambiguous mission context. Mitigation: specify a formal schema for worksite mission context and validate all inputs.

RISK: If the approval workflow (Site Manager gate) is not integrated into the PRE engine, baselines may be recorded without proper authorization. Mitigation: implement approval as a mandatory state in the baseline lifecycle.
RISK: If the audit log is not properly isolated or protected, it may be modified or deleted, compromising traceability. Mitigation: use append-only storage and role-based access control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define a Performance Management subsystem within NGHE Solution with explicit ownership (e.g., System Engineering team). Specify the internal architecture: PRE engine (derivation logic), Baseline Store (versioned database), Approval Workflow (state machine), and Audit Log (append-only). Ensure all external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are formally specified with schemas and protocols. Document the approval SLA and escalation path.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (DP-D: Hybrid PRE + approval + audit log) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) PRE engine ingests mission context (FR 005.1), (2) applies derivation rules (FR 005.2), (3) records baselines with metadata (FR 005.3), (4) maintains audit history (FR 005.4), (5) provides traceability (FR 005.5). The DP is complete, feasible, and aligned with high safety criticality (approval gate + audit trail). No gaps or mismatches detected.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (Low): The PRE engine logic may be duplicated if Vehicle modules attempt to derive their own performance targets. Mitigation: enforce that all derivation goes through the centralized NGHE Solution PRE engine; Vehicle modules only consume derived targets via Telemetry interface.

DIFFUSION RISK (Low): The approval workflow may be scattered across multiple portals (Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Manager). Mitigation: centralize approval in a single NGHE Solution portal accessible to authorized roles.
DIFFUSION RISK (Low): The audit log may be replicated across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for local caching. Mitigation: use a read-only cache with a single source of truth in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module provides strong containment for the PRE engine and baseline management. All derivation logic, persistence, and approval workflow are internal. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not leak implementation details. Containment score of 95 is justified. No architectural diffusion detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible and traceable. The NGHE Solution module can be tested in isolation: (a) unit tests for PRE derivation logic (given mission context, verify correct performance targets); (b) integration tests for baseline recording and approval workflow (given Site Manager approval, verify baseline is recorded with metadata); (c) audit trail tests (verify all updates are logged with who, what, when, why); (d) traceability tests (verify links from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history). All tests can be automated and traced to acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear. The NGHE Solution module owns the entire CR-NGHE-005 requirement. Sub-FRs are allocated to internal components: PRE engine (005.1, 005.2), Baseline Store (005.3, 005.4), Approval Workflow (005.3), Audit Log (005.4), Traceability Service (005.5). Each component has a clear responsibility and interface. No ambiguity or overlap detected.

recommended_revisions: Formalize the Performance Management subsystem architecture within NGHE Solution (PRE engine, Baseline Store, Approval Workflow, Audit Log, Traceability Service).
Specify the Tasking interface schema for worksite mission context (e.g., JSON schema with terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints).
Specify the Telemetry interface schema for derived performance requirements (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins).
Define the approval workflow SLA (e.g., Site Manager must review and approve within 24 hours) and escalation path (e.g., if Site Manager unavailable, escalate to Project Manager).
Specify the audit log schema and immutability guarantees (e.g., append-only database, cryptographic hashing for integrity).
Define role-based access control for the approval portal (Site Manager, Safety Manager, System Engineer, Maintenance/Quality).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR has five sub-FRs (005.1 through 005.5) that address distinct functional concerns: mission context ingestion, derivation logic, baseline recording, history management, and traceability. Each sub-FR requires its own FR-DP-IM decomposition to ensure completeness and traceability. The selected DP (DP-D) is sound and the assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate, but the next level must detail how each sub-FR is realized within the Performance Management subsystem. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify the internal architecture and ensure all acceptance criteria are met.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall ingest and validate worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints, operational policies) from the Tasking interface.

CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context using defined derivation rules and shall validate computed requirements against feasibility constraints.

CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver) and shall persist the baseline to a versioned store.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why, approval status).
CR-NGHE-005.5: The NGHE solution shall provide traceability links from worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline records → history entries and shall expose traceability via query interface.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in a structured format (e.g., JSON) via the Tasking interface.
Derivation rules (e.g., cycle time = f(terrain, payload, vehicle capability)) are well-defined and can be encoded as deterministic logic.
Site Manager has authority and availability to review and approve derived requirements in a timely manner.
The NGHE Solution module has sufficient computational and storage capacity to run the PRE engine and maintain the audit log.
Network Link provides reliable communication between NGHE Solution and Site Manager portal and central repository.
Safety criticality is high, so approval gate and audit trail are mandatory (not optional).
open_risks: RISK: Derivation rules may be incomplete or incorrect, leading to infeasible or unsafe requirements. Mitigation: validation rules and Site Manager review gate.
RISK: Site Manager approval workflow may become a bottleneck. Mitigation: define SLA and escalation path; consider automated approval for low-risk updates.
RISK: Audit log may grow very large over time, impacting performance. Mitigation: implement log rotation and archival strategy.
RISK: PRE engine may not scale to new worksite types or mission contexts. Mitigation: design with rule parameterization and flexible schema.
RISK: Traceability links may be broken if baselines or history records are deleted. Mitigation: enforce referential integrity and use append-only storage.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of Tasking interface schema (worksite mission context).
Formal specification of derivation rules and validation criteria.
Formal specification of Telemetry interface schema (derived performance requirements).
Formal specification of approval workflow (SLA, escalation path, roles).
Formal specification of audit log schema and immutability guarantees.
Formal specification of traceability query interface.
Prototype or proof-of-concept of PRE engine with sample mission contexts.
Risk assessment for high safety criticality (e.g., FMEA for derivation logic failures).
Acceptance test plan with test cases for each sub-FR.
